findings: Most misses still ranked the actual winner near the front; prioritize probability/ranking calibration before large model rewrites.
Race-pace priors frequently favored the wrong top pick; review FastF1 form weighting, team baseline strength, and track-affinity scaling.
Qualifying/grid priors favored the wrong top pick in some misses; add session-specific qualifying features before increasing grid influence.
The traceable BeliefState favored at least one wrong top pick; inspect state-update provenance, confidence gates, and route scales before treating the current state as calibrated truth.
Most miss reviews still lack same-time market priors, so model-vs-market edge diagnosis remains incomplete.

events.review_summary: Australian Grand Prix was a replay hit; keep it as a control row when testing model changes.
events.next_action: Use as a control row in matched simulator ablations.

events.review_summary: Barcelona Grand Prix missed with russell over hamilton; diagnosis: actual_winner_underweighted, race_pace_prior_favored_top_pick, grid_prior_favored_top_pick, belief_state_favored_top_pick, no_market_prior_to_compare.
